records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4164 - Ibn al-Mughira narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdillah (as), that Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in eating what is slaughtered with a stone if no iron is available."
records.arabicText: 4164 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تَأْكُلَ مَا ذُبِحَ بِحَجَرٍ إِذَا لَمْ تَجِدْ حَدِيدَةً".

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4165 - Al-Fadl and Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said: "A group of people came to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) and said to him: 'A cow of ours overpowered us and became difficult to handle, so we struck it with a sword.'
Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) instructed them to eat it."
records.arabicText: 4165 - وَ رَوَى اَلْفَضْلُ وَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ قَوْماً أَتَوُا اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالُوا لَهُ إِنَّ بَقَرَةً لَنَا غَلَبَتْنَا وَ اِسْتَصْعَبَتْ عَلَيْنَا فَضَرَبْنَاهَا بِالسَّيْفِ فَأَمَرَهُمْ بِأَكْلِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4165 - Al-Fadl and Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A group of people came to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) and said to him: 'A cow of ours overpowered us and became difficult to handle, so we struck it with a sword.'
Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) instructed them to eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4166 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Al-Is ibn al-Qasim, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said: "A bull in Kufa became wild, and the people attacked it with their swords and struck it. They then came to Commander of the Faithful (as) and asked him about it.
Imam (as) said: 'It is a valid slaughtering, and its meat is lawful.'"
records.arabicText: 4166 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنِ اَلْعِيصِ بْنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِنَّ ثَوْراً ثَارَ بِالْكُوفَةِ فَثَارَ إِلَيْهِ اَلنَّاسُ بِأَسْيَافِهِمْ فَضَرَبُوهُ وَ أَتَوْا أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَسَأَلُوهُ فَقَالَ "ذَكَاةٌ وَحِيَّةٌ وَ لَحْمُهُ حَلاَلٌ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4166 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Al-Is ibn al-Qasim, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said: "A bull in Kufa became wild, and the people attacked it with their swords and struck it. They then came to Commander of the Faithful (as) and asked him about it.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'It is a valid slaughtering, and its meat is lawful.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4167 - Aban narrated from Zurara, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a camel that fell into a well and was slaughtered from the side of its tail.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it if they mentioned the name of Allah (swt) over it."
records.arabicText: 4167 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ بَعِيرٍ تَرَدَّى فِي بِئْرٍ فَذُبِحَ مِنْ قِبَلِ ذَنَبِهِ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ إِذَا ذَكَرُوا اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4167 - Aban narrated from Zurara, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a camel that fell into a well and was slaughtered from the side of its tail.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it if they mentioned the name of Allah (swt) over it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4168 - Umar ibn Udhaynah narrated from Al-Fudayl who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who was slaughtering an animal, and the knife moved too quickly and severed the head completely.
Imam (as) said: "It is a valid slaughtering, and there is no harm in eating it."
records.arabicText: 4168 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ ذَبَحَ فَسَبَقَهُ اَلسِّكِّينُ فَقَطَعَ اَلرَّأْسَ فَقَالَ "ذَكَاةٌ وَحِيَّةٌ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِأَكْلِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4168 - Umar ibn Udhaynah narrated from Al-Fudayl who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who was slaughtering an animal, and the knife moved too quickly and severed the head completely.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "It is a valid slaughtering, and there is no harm in eating it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4169 - In the narration of Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), he said: "If the blood flows, then eat."
records.arabicText: 4169 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِنْ خَرَجَ اَلدَّمُ فَكُلْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4169 - In the narration of Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the blood flows, then eat."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4170 - In the narration of Samaah from Abu Abdillah (as), Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it if the blood flows."
records.arabicText: 4170 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ إِذَا سَالَ اَلدَّمُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4170 - In the narration of Samaah from Abu Abdillah (as), Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it if the blood flows."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4171 - Abu Basir asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a sheep that is slaughtered but does not move, yet a lot of fresh blood flows from it.
Imam (as) said: "Do not eat it. Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say: 'If the leg kicks or the eye blinks, then eat it.'"
records.arabicText: 4171 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلشَّاةِ تُذْبَحُ فَلاَ تَتَحَرَّكُ وَ يُهَرَاقُ مِنْهَا دَمٌ كَثِيرٌ عَبِيطٌ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَأْكُلْ إِنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ يَقُولُ "إِذَا رَكَضَتِ اَلرِّجْلُ أَوْ طَرَفَتِ اَلْعَيْنُ فَكُلْ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4171 - Abu Basir asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a sheep that is slaughtered but does not move, yet a lot of fresh blood flows from it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Do not eat it. Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say: 'If the leg kicks or the eye blinks, then eat it.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4172 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (as), that he was asked about a man who slaughtered a bird and severed its head.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, it may be eaten, but he should not deliberately cut off its head."
records.arabicText: 4172 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ ذَبَحَ طَيْراً فَقَطَعَ رَأْسَهُ أَ يُؤْكَلُ مِنْهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ وَ لَكِنْ لاَ يَتَعَمَّدْ قَطْعَ رَأْسِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4172 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdillah (as), that he was asked about a man who slaughtered a bird and severed its head.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, it may be eaten, but he should not deliberately cut off its head."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4173 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
"Do not eat from what has been killed by a predator, nor from that which has been beaten to death, strangled, fallen from a height, or gored, unless you find it alive and slaughter it properly."
records.arabicText: 4173 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ تَأْكُلَنَّ مِنْ فَرِيسَةِ اَلسَّبُعِ وَ لاَ اَلْمَوْقُوذَةِ وَ لاَ اَلْمُنْخَنِقَةِ وَ لاَ اَلْمُتَرَدِّيَةِ وَ لاَ اَلنَّطِيحَةِ إِلاَّ أَنْ تُدْرِكَهُ حَيّاً فَتُذَكِّيَهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4173 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not eat from what has been killed by a predator, nor from that which has been beaten to death, strangled, fallen from a height, or gored, unless you find it alive and slaughter it properly."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4174 - Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he said regarding an animal that is slaughtered and has a fetus in its womb: "If it is fully formed, then eat it, for its slaughtering is the slaughtering of its mother. But if it is not fully formed, then do not eat it."
records.arabicText: 4174 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : فِي اَلذَّبِيحَةِ تُذْبَحُ وَ فِي بَطْنِهَا وَلَدٌ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ تَامّاً فَكُلْهُ فَإِنَّ ذَكَاتَهُ ذَكَاةُ أُمِّهِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ تَامّاً فَلاَ تَأْكُلْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4174 - Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he said regarding an animal that is slaughtered and has a fetus in its womb:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it is fully formed, then eat it, for its slaughtering is the slaughtering of its mother. But if it is not fully formed, then do not eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4175 - Umar ibn Udhaynah narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (peace be upon them), that he said: I asked him about the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic: "Lawful to you are the beasts of cattle." (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:1)
Imam (as) said: "The fetus, if it has grown hair or fur, its slaughtering is the slaughtering of its mother."
records.arabicText: 4175 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: ۝ أُحِلَّتْ لَكُمْ بَهِيمَةُ اَلْأَنْعٰامِ ۝ فَقَالَ "اَلْجَنِينُ إِذَا أَشْعَرَ أَوْ أَوْبَرَ فَذَكَاتُهُ ذَكَاةُ أُمِّهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4175 - Umar ibn Udhaynah narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (peace be upon them), that he said: I asked him about the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic: "Lawful to you are the beasts of cattle." (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:1)
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The fetus, if it has grown hair or fur, its slaughtering is the slaughtering of its mother."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4176 - Al-Kahili narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that a man asked him a question while I was present about cutting the tails of sheep.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in cutting them if you are doing so to improve your wealth."
Then Imam (as) said: "Indeed, in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written: 'Whatever is cut from a living animal is considered dead and should not be used.'"
records.arabicText: 4176 - وَ رَوَى اَلْكَاهِلِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ وَ أَنَا عِنْدَهُ عَنْ قَطْعِ أَلَيَاتِ اَلْغَنَمِ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِقَطْعِهَا إِذَا كُنْتَ إِنَّمَا تُصْلِحُ بِهِ مَالَكَ" ثُمَّ قَالَ "إِنَّ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَنَّ مَا قُطِعَ مِنْهَا مَيْتٌ لاَ يُنْتَفَعُ بِهِ " ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4176 - Al-Kahili narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that a man asked him a question while I was present about cutting the tails of sheep.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in cutting them if you are doing so to improve your wealth."
Then Imam (as) said: "Indeed, in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written: 'Whatever is cut from a living animal is considered dead and should not be used.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4177 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Every animal that should be slaughtered but is instead killed by stabbing is unlawful, and every animal that should be killed by stabbing but is instead slaughtered is unlawful."
records.arabicText: 4177 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلُّ مَنْحُورٍ مَذْبُوحٍ حَرَامٌ وَ كُلُّ مَذْبُوحٍ مَنْحُورٍ حَرَامٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4177 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every animal that should be slaughtered but is instead killed by stabbing is unlawful, and every animal that should be killed by stabbing but is instead slaughtered is unlawful."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4178 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated that Al-Marzuban asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about the slaughtered animal of a child born out of wedlock, and they were certain of his status.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, and it is permissible for a woman and a child if they are in necessity."
records.arabicText: 4178 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى قَالَ سَأَلَ اَلْمَرْزُبَانُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ ذَبِيحَةِ وَلَدِ اَلزِّنَا وَ قَدْ عَرَفْنَاهُ بِذَلِكَ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ وَ اَلصَّبِيُّ إِذَا اُضْطُرُّوا إِلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4178 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated that Al-Marzuban asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about the slaughtered animal of a child born out of wedlock, and they were certain of his status.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, and it is permissible for a woman and a child if they are in necessity."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4179 - Al-Halabi asked him about the slaughtered animal of the Murji'ite and the Haruri. Imam (as) said: "Eat, and be at ease and settled until whatever is to happen occurs."
records.arabicText: 4179 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنْ ذَبِيحَةِ اَلْمُرْجِئِ وَ اَلْحَرُورِيِّ قَالَ فَقَالَ "كُلْ وَ قِرَّ وَ اِسْتَقِرَّ حَتَّى يَكُونَ مَا يَكُونُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4179 - Al-Halabi asked him about the slaughtered animal of the Murji'ite and the Haruri. Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat, and be at ease and settled until whatever is to happen occurs."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4180 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not eat the slaughtered animal of a Jew, a Christian, a Magian, or anyone who opposes the religion, except if you hear him mention the name of Allah (swt) over it."
And in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written: "A Magian, a Christian, or the Christians of the Arabs should not slaughter the sacrificial animals."
Imam (as) also said: "You may eat their slaughtered animal if they mention the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, over it."
records.arabicText: 4180 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ تَأْكُلْ ذَبِيحَةَ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ وَ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ وَ جَمِيعِ مَنْ خَالَفَ اَلدِّينَ إِلاَّ مَا إِذَا سَمِعْتَهُ يَذْكُرُ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهَا وَ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لاَ يَذْبَحِ اَلْمَجُوسِيُّ وَ لاَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيُّ وَ لاَ نَصَارَى اَلْعَرَبِ اَلْأَضَاحِيَّ " " وَ قَالَ "تَأْكُلُ ذَبِيحَتَهُ إِذَا ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4180 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do not eat the slaughtered animal of a Jew, a Christian, a Magian, or anyone who opposes the religion, except if you hear him mention the name of Allah (swt) over it."
And in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written: "A Magian, a Christian, or the Christians of the Arabs should not slaughter the sacrificial animals."
Imam (as) also said: "You may eat their slaughtered animal if they mention the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, over it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4181 - And in the narration of Abdul Malik bin Amr, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"I asked him, 'What do you say about the slaughtered animals of the Christians?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm in them.'
I said: 'But they mention the name of the Messiah over them.'
Imam (as) replied: 'They only mean Allah (swt), the Exalted, by the Messiah.'"
records.arabicText: 4181 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا تَقُولُ فِي ذَبَائِحِ اَلنَّصَارَى فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهَا" قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُمْ يَذْكُرُونَ عَلَيْهَا اَلْمَسِيحَ فَقَالَ "إِنَّمَا أَرَادُوا بِالْمَسِيحِ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4181 - And in the narration of Abdul Malik bin Amr, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him, 'What do you say about the slaughtered animals of the Christians?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm in them.'
I said: 'But they mention the name of the Messiah over them.'
Imam (as) replied: 'They only mean Allah (swt), the Exalted, by the Messiah.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4182 - And Abu Bakr al-Hadrami narrated from Al-Ward bin Zaid, who said:
I said to Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), 'Relate to me a narration and dictate it to me so that I may write it down.'
Imam (as) said: 'Where is your memory, O people of Kufa?'
I said: 'So that no one disputes it with me later.'
I then asked: 'What do you say about a Magian who says 'In the name of Allah (swt)' and slaughters an animal?'
Imam (as) said: 'Eat.'
I said: 'What if a Muslim slaughters but does not mention the name of Allah (swt)?'
Imam (as) replied: 'Do not eat. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Exalted, says:
'So eat of that upon which the name of Allah (swt) has been mentioned' (Surah Al-An'am 6:118)
and He (swt) also says:
'And do not eat of that upon which the name of Allah (swt) has not been mentioned.' (Surah Al-An'am 6:121)'"
records.arabicText: 4182 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَكْرٍ اَلْحَضْرَمِيُّ عَنِ اَلْوَرْدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حَدِّثْنِي حَدِيثاً وَ أَمْلِ عَلَيَّ حَتَّى أَكْتُبَهُ فَقَالَ "أَيْنَ حِفْظُكُمْ يَا أَهْلَ اَلْكُوفَةِ " قُلْتُ حَتَّى لاَ يَرُدَّهُ عَلَيَّ أَحَدٌ، مَا تَقُولُ فِي مَجُوسِيٍّ قَالَ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ وَ ذَبَحَ فَقَالَ "كُلْ" فَقُلْتُ مُسْلِمٌ ذَبَحَ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَأْكُلْ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى يَقُولُ: ۝ فَكُلُوا مِمّٰا ذُكِرَ اِسْمُ اَللّٰهِ عَلَيْهِ ۝ وَ يَقُولُ ۝ وَ لاٰ تَأْكُلُوا مِمّٰا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اِسْمُ اَللّٰهِ عَلَيْهِ ۝ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4182 - And Abu Bakr al-Hadrami narrated from Al-Ward bin Zaid, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), 'Relate to me a narration and dictate it to me so that I may write it down.'
Imam (as) said: 'Where is your memory, O people of Kufa?'
I said: 'So that no one disputes it with me later.'
I then asked: 'What do you say about a Magian who says 'In the name of Allah (swt)' and slaughters an animal?'
Imam (as) said: 'Eat.'
I said: 'What if a Muslim slaughters but does not mention the name of Allah (swt)?'
Imam (as) replied: 'Do not eat. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Exalted, says:
'So eat of that upon which the name of Allah (swt) has been mentioned' (Surah Al-An'am 6:118)
and He (swt) also says:
'And do not eat of that upon which the name of Allah (swt) has not been mentioned.' (Surah Al-An'am 6:121)'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4183 - And Al-Husayn Al-Ahmasi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"It is the (mentioning of) the name, and none is to be trusted with it except a Muslim."
records.arabicText: 4183 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ اَلْأَحْمَسِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "هُوَ اَلاِسْمُ وَ لاَ يُؤْمَنُ عَلَيْهِ إِلاَّ مُسْلِمٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4183 - And Al-Husayn Al-Ahmasi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is the (mentioning of) the name, and none is to be trusted with it except a Muslim."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4184 - Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Ubayd Allah (swt) who said: I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "We are in the mountains and we send the shepherds to the sheep. Sometimes a sheep is injured or something happens to it, and they slaughter it. Can we eat it?"
Imam (as) said: "No, for it is a matter of proper slaughtering, and only a Muslim can be trusted with it."
records.arabicText: 4184 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ اَلْمُخْتَارِ عَنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اَللَّهِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّا نَكُونُ بِالْجَبَلِ فَنَبْعَثُ اَلرُّعَاةَ إِلَى اَلْغَنَمِ فَرُبَّمَا عَطِبَتِ اَلشَّاةُ وَ أَصَابَهَا شَيْءٌ فَذَبَحُوهَا فَنَأْكُلُهَا قَالَ "لاَ إِنَّمَا هِيَ اَلذَّبِيحَةُ فَلاَ يُؤْمَنُ عَلَيْهَا إِلاَّ اَلْمُسْلِمُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4184 - Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Ubayd Allah (swt) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "We are in the mountains and we send the shepherds to the sheep. Sometimes a sheep is injured or something happens to it, and they slaughter it. Can we eat it?"
Imam (as) said: "No, for it is a matter of proper slaughtering, and only a Muslim can be trusted with it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4185 - And it is narrated from Al-Fudayl, Zurara, and Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
They asked him about buying meat from the markets without knowing what the butchers do.
Imam (as) said: "Eat if it is in the markets of the Muslims, and do not ask about it."
records.arabicText: 4185 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ وَ زُرَارَةَ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُمْ سَأَلُوهُ عَنْ شِرَاءِ اَللَّحْمِ مِنَ اَلْأَسْوَاقِ وَ لاَ يُدْرَى مَا يَصْنَعُ اَلْقَصَّابُونَ فَقَالَ "كُلْ إِذَا كَانَ فِي أَسْوَاقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ لاَ تَسْأَلْ عَنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4185 - And it is narrated from Al-Fudayl, Zurara, and Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
They asked him about buying meat from the markets without knowing what the butchers do.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat if it is in the markets of the Muslims, and do not ask about it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4186 - And Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Abdullah (as), about an animal that was slaughtered not facing the Qibla.
Imam (as) said: "Eat, there is no harm in that as long as it was not done intentionally."
He further asked Imam (as) about a man who slaughtered an animal without mentioning the name of Allah (swt).
Imam (as) said: "If he forgot, let him mention the name of Allah (swt) when he remembers and say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), at its beginning and at its end.'"
records.arabicText: 4186 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ ذَبِيحَةٍ ذُبِحَتْ لِغَيْرِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ فَقَالَ "كُلْ لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ مَا لَمْ يُتَعَمَّدْ" قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ ذَبَحَ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ نَاسِياً فَلْيُسَمِّ حِينَ يَذْكُرُ يَقُولُ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ عَلَى أَوَّلِهِ وَ عَلَى آخِرِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4186 - And Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Abdullah (as), about an animal that was slaughtered not facing the Qibla.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Eat, there is no harm in that as long as it was not done intentionally."
He further asked Imam (as) about a man who slaughtered an animal without mentioning the name of Allah (swt).
Imam (as) said: "If he forgot, let him mention the name of Allah (swt) when he remembers and say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), at its beginning and at its end.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4187 - And Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a man who slaughtered an animal while saying Subhan Allah (swt) (Glory be to Allah (swt)), or Allahu Akbar (Allah (swt) is the Greatest), or La Ilaha Illa Allah (swt) (There is no god but Allah (swt)), or Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah (swt)).
Imam (as) said: "All of these are among the names of Allah (swt), the Exalted, and there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4187 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ ذَبَحَ فَسَبَّحَ أَوْ كَبَّرَ أَوْ هَلَّلَ أَوْ حَمَّدَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَالَ "هَذَا كُلُّهُ مِنْ أَسْمَاءِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4187 - And Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), about a man who slaughtered an animal while saying Subhan Allah (swt) (Glory be to Allah (swt)), or Allahu Akbar (Allah (swt) is the Greatest), or La Ilaha Illa Allah (swt) (There is no god but Allah (swt)), or Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah (swt)).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "All of these are among the names of Allah (swt), the Exalted, and there is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4188 - And in the narration of Hammad, from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as), it is reported:
Imam (as) was asked about a man who slaughters an animal but forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt), can his slaughtered animal be eaten?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he is not suspected (of negligence) and is known to perform slaughtering properly, and if he does not sever the spinal cord or break the neck until the animal has completely cooled (ceased movement)."
records.arabicText: 4188 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادٍ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَذْبَحُ فَيَنْسَى أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ أَ تُؤْكَلُ ذَبِيحَتُهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ إِذَا كَانَ لاَ يُتَّهَمُ وَ يُحْسِنُ اَلذَّبْحَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ وَ لاَ يَنْخَعُ وَ لاَ يَكْسِرُ اَلرَّقَبَةَ حَتَّى تَبْرُدَ اَلذَّبِيحَةُ ".
